source: ESPN FIFA World Cup + per-player news feeds

items.headline: Senegal striker Pape Gueye quits team until technical staff is replaced, following FIFA World Cup exit
items.description: Pape Gueye has indefinitely quit the Senegal national team in the aftermath of their FIFA World Cup exit against Belgium on Wednesday, revealing that he will only return to the fold when the current technical team are replaced.

items.headline: Belgium's Youri Tielemans downplays Leandro Trossard clash: 'Part of football'
items.description: Youri Tielemans has played down talks of a rift with teammate Leandro Trossard following their on-field scuffle during Belgium's round-of-32 World Cup win against Senegal.

items.headline: Ghana's Brandon Thomas-Asante showing why he belongs in the Premier League with Coventry
items.description: The Black Stars forward's journey has taken him from non-league football and the lower reaches of the English pyramid to the Premier League with Coventry City and now, to the biggest stage of all - representing Ghana in the World Cup knockouts.

items.headline: Cristian Romero available for Argentina World Cup knockout vs. Cape Verde - sources
items.description: Argentina have received a boost ahead of Friday's World Cup round of 32 encounter against Cape Verde in Miami with Cristian Romero available.

items.headline: World Cup Daily live: Balogun celly in USMNT win gets LeBron seal of approval
items.description: Follow live for the latest analysis, reaction and news as the FIFA World Cup round of 32 continues.

items.headline: Thomas Tuchel: No signs of England shirt weighing down players at World Cup
items.description: Thomas Tuchel saw spirit rather than any hint of the England shirt weighing heavy as Harry Kane's brace secured a memorable World Cup comeback win against the Democratic Republic of Congo.

items.headline: Portugal's Vitinha urges fans to keep faith ahead of Croatia clash

items.description: Tottenham Hotspur have announced the signing of midfielder Mateus Fernandes from West Ham.

items.headline: From Chelsea wonderkid to Black Stars elder statesman - Baba Rahman's years of recovery lead to Kansas City
items.description: Once the bright young thing of the Ghana setup, Abdul Rahman Baba's career trajectory was transformed by a devastating injury suffered in 2017, but now back in the Black Stars fold, he's relishing the role of elder statesman with the national side.

items.headline: World Cup VAR review: Misapplied protocols leave Balogun wrongly red-carded
items.description: Folarin Balogun was sent off in the USMNT's 2-0 win over Bosnia-Herzegovina on Wednesday, but as our former referee explains, he shouldn't have been.

items.headline: USMNT shows it can play ugly at this World Cup, which is exactly what it needs to succeed
items.description: Play pretty? Fine. Play nasty? No problem. This USMNT has the tools to deal with every situation, which is handy as it faces Belgium next without its top scorer.

items.headline: Colombia vs. Ghana - Kick-off time, team news, how to watch World Cup round of 32 clash
items.description: Ghana will face Colombia in a World Cup round of 32 clash in Kansas City on Friday as the Black Stars look to bounce back from their Croatia loss.

items.headline: Portugal ready to honor World Cup's 'extra man' Diogo Jota as anniversary of his death nears
items.description: Portugal forward Diogo Jota died in a car accident a year ago, so when the country take to the pitch against Croatia on Thursday it will be an emotional time.

items.headline: South Africa's Bafana Bafana arrive home to heroes' welcome after FIFA World Cup

items.headline: U.S. beats Bosnia-Herzegovina to reach World Cup round of 16
items.description: Folarin Balogun scored his third goal of the World Cup before being sent off with a red card, and Malik Tillman converted on a free kick to give the 10-man U.S. squad a 2-0 win over Bosnia-Herzegovina on Wednesday night to advance to the round of 16.
